title: Allow Multiple-to-One Field Mapping in Migration
story: As an end user, I want to map one import field to multiple Salesforce fields, so that I can handle scenarios where the same source data feeds into different target fields.
description:
Remove the restriction that prevented mapping one import field to multiple Salesforce fields. A previous version displayed a JavaScript error when a user attempted this, but multiple-to-one mapping is a valid use case.

### Context
There are numerous scenarios where a single source field needs to map to multiple Salesforce destination fields. The old JavaScript validation error was overly restrictive and should be removed to support these workflows.
acceptance_criteria: A single import field can be mapped to multiple Salesforce fields without error
No JavaScript validation error is thrown on duplicate source field selection
Existing single-field mappings continue to work as before
ux_changes: JavaScript validation that blocked duplicate source field selection is removed, allowing users to assign the same import field to multiple target fields.
middleware_changes: Backend mapping logic updated to accept and process multiple target fields for a single source field.
implementation_notes: This feature was likely already implemented before the ticket was formally created. Developer confirmed the capability existed.
comment_summary: Developer noted the feature appeared to already be implemented and asked for confirmation.
